zh.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica是Alberto di Massa和Calcerana di Curte的兒子，於大約1090年出生。他和Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa至少有1個兒子。他去世於1155年在義大利享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica是Alberto di Massa和Calcerana di Curte的儿子，于大约1090年出生。他和Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa至少有1个儿子。他去世于1155年在意大利享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Marchese
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Angelo, Rocco, Sal, Antonio, Carlo, Giuseppe, Santo, Donato, Franco, Gaetano.

Italian: from the title of rank marchése ‘marquis’ (in Italy the rank immediately below that of duke), probably applied as a nickname for someone who behaved like a marquis, or an occupational name for a servant in the household of a marquis. However, 13th- and 14th-century documents show that the term was also used as a personal name at that time.

variants.name: Marchione
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Rocco, Angelo, Vito, Adriana, Aldo, Armando, Evo, Pasquale, Rodolfo, Romolo, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): nickname from marchione ‘marquis’, from medieval Latin marchio, genitive marchionis, from ancient Germanic marka ‘borderland’. See also Marchese .

variants.name: Marchesi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Giancarlo, Gino, Mario, Aldo, Angelo, Annamarie, Carlo, Ennio, Franco, Giuseppe, Guido, Lorenzo, Luigi, Renato, Sergio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Marchese  .

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Massa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Massey
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Massaro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Pasquale, Rocco, Antonio, Gennaro, Vito, Carlo, Gaetano, Nunzio, Antoninette.

Italian: occupational or status name from massaro, which in northern Italy denoted a tenant farmer or share cropper and in central and southern Italy could also denote an agent or steward of a rural estate, from medieval Latin massarius, a derivative of massa ‘farm, estate’. Compare Massa and Massara .

nameMeanings.name: e
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鄂, a short form of present-day Hubei province: (i) from E (鄂), the name of an ancient state (located in Henan province), destroyed by the king Zhou, last king of the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) from the placename E (鄂), the name of a fief (located in Shanxi province) in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the placename E (鄂), the name of a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to the royal members of the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) possibly a surname from minority ethnic groups in southwestern China. (v) said to be adopted as a new surname by Yue Ting, who changed his original name Yue (嶽) to E (鄂), because he fled to Huangmei in present-day Hubei province (called E (鄂) at that time) to avoid persecution after his father Yue Fei (1103–1142  AD ), a famous Chinese general in the Southern Song dynasty (1127 – 1279  AD ), was put to death by the then emperor.

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Oberto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Robert .

en.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica was born about 1090 as the son of Alberto di Massa and Calcerana di Curte. He had at least 1 son with Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa. He died in 1155, in Italy, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica est né environ 1090, fils de Alberto di Massa et Calcerana di Curte. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa. Il est décédé en 1155, à Italie, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica wurde um 1090, als Sohn von Alberto di Massa und Calcerana di Curte geboren. Er hatte mit Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1155 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Italien.

it.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica è nato il circa 1090 come figlio di Alberto di Massa e Calcerana di Curte. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa. Morì nel 1155, nel Italia, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsicaは約1090年にAlberto di MassaとCalcerana di Curteの息子として生まれた。彼はGiorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1155年にイタリアで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica(은)는 대략 1090년 Alberto di Massa(와)과 Calcerana di Curte의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1155년 이탈리아에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica nasceu aproximadamente 1090 filho de Alberto di Massa e Calcerana di Curte. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa. Ele faleceu em 1155, em Itália, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica родился около 1090 г. Его родителей звали Alberto di Massa и Calcerana di Curte. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa. Он умер в 1155 г. в местности Италия в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Oberto Obertenghi Marchese di Massa e di Corsica nació aproximadamente 1090 como hijo de Alberto di Massa y Calcerana di Curte. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Giorgia di Lacon-Gunale, Marchioness of Massa. Murió en 1155, en Italia, a la edad de 66 años.
